channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Em evento nos EUA, Flávio Bolsonaro pede pressão diplomática para que eleições tenham 'valores de origem americana'
item.description: At a conservative event in Texas, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro urged the U.S. to apply diplomatic pressure to ensure free and fair elections in Brazil in 2026, based on 'American-origin values'. He accused the Biden administration of interfering in the 2022 elections, compared his father Jair Bolsonaro’s legal situation to 'lawfare', and called the former president Trump’s most loyal international ally. Flávio emphasized Brazil’s strategic importance in the Western Hemisphere due to its territory, population, economy, and critical mineral reserves.

item.title: Trumpismo de Flávio Bolsonaro fragiliza lero-lero da moderação
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro presents himself as a moderate figure of Bolsonarism, emphasizing marketing over a substantive government plan. He states that while he consults his father, Jair Bolsonaro, for decisions, he is the actual candidate, attempting to distance himself from the polarizing figure of the former president.

item.title: Único resultado da CPI foi jogar Lulinha no colo de Lula em pleno ano eleitoral
item.description: The INSS CPMI ended without substantive results, with its only practical effect being the political exposure of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva (Lulinha), which inadvertently boosted Lula’s campaign by shifting focus away from Jair Bolsonaro’s culpability. The commission was marked by partisan maneuvers, such as the relator Alfredo Gaspar’s last-minute affiliation with the PL party, and serious allegations against him, including alleged rape of a minor, that were not pursued. The Planalto’s alternative report was rejected, and the CPMI failed to address the victims of the INSS fraud scheme, underscoring Congress’s prioritization of political games over accountability.

item.title: Roberto Carneiro assume Secretaria de Governo em São Paulo
item.description: Roberto Carneiro, São Paulo's Chief of Staff, will assume the State Government Secretariat, replacing Gilberto Kassab, who stepped down to organize the PSD for the 2026 elections. The appointment, to be published in the Official Gazette on March 30, is part of Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' strategy to centralize political articulation. Nerylson Lima will replace Carneiro as Chief of Staff.

item.title: RJ: após liminar de Zanin, centro, direita e esquerda apoiam eleição direta
item.description: Following a temporary injunction by Supreme Court Justice Cristiano Zanin that halted the indirect election for Rio de Janeiro's governor, leaders from center, right, and left-wing factions took to social media to advocate for a direct vote. This rare cross-ideological consensus underscores widespread concern over institutional instability in the state.

item.title: Moro critica “vergonhosa atuação” do governo Lula na CPMI do INSS
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro criticized the 'shameful performance' of the Lula government in the INSS CPMI, accusing the government bloc of trying to block investigations by rejecting the final report. He claimed the government sought to approve a separate vote that would protect President Lula’s son, Lulinha. Moro views this as an attempt to obstruct the inquiry into alleged irregularities.

item.title: Na CPAC, Flávio compara Bolsonaro a Trump e associa Lula a facções
item.description: At CPAC 2026, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro compared his father Jair Bolsonaro’s political trajectory to that of Donald Trump, accusing the Lula government of favoring criminal factions like the Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC) and Comando Vermelho, and pursuing a foreign policy aligned with China, Cuba, and Iran against U.S. interests. Without providing evidence, he claimed Lula lobbies for criminal groups and advocated for a realignment of Brazil with Washington. The speech also denounced Latin American leftism as an organized ideological bloc and predicted a right-wing victory in Brazil’s 2026 election.

item.title: Nos EUA, Flávio pede monitoramento e pressão diplomática por 'eleições justas' no Brasil
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) urged the United States to monitor freedom of expression in Brazil and apply diplomatic pressure to ensure fair elections in October, the year he plans to run for President of Brazil.

item.title: Nos EUA, Flávio pede monitoramento e pressão diplomática por ‘eleições justas’ no Brasil
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) spoke at CPAC in the U.S., urging American monitoring and diplomatic pressure on Brazil’s 2026 elections to ensure 'free and fair' voting. He accused the Biden administration of interfering in Brazil’s 2022 elections via alleged USAID funding for Lula’s campaign, claiming it caused economic crisis, cartel expansion, and corruption, while comparing his father’s legal persecution to that of Donald Trump.

item.title: Nos EUA, Flávio defende pressão externa por “eleições justas” no Brasil
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro urged the international community during a speech at CPAC in the United States to closely monitor freedom of expression in Brazil and apply diplomatic pressure to ensure fair elections. Positioning himself as a pre-candidate for president, he advocated for greater external scrutiny of Brazilian institutions.

item.title: Na CPAC, Flávio compara pai a Trump e acusa Lula de 'lobby por CV e PCC'
item.description: Speaking at CPAC in Dallas, Flávio Bolsonaro compared his father, Jair Bolsonaro, to Donald Trump and accused President Lula of promoting a 'lobby' in favor of criminal factions CV and PCC. He framed the 2026 Brazilian presidential election as an existential battle for the American right, seeking to mobilize international support for his father's candidacy.

item.title: Nos EUA, Flávio Bolsonaro apela por pressão sobre eleições no Brasil
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, senator and pre-candidate for presidency, appealed at CPAC in Dallas for the U.S. to exert diplomatic pressure on Brazil's 2026 elections, claiming Lula is a 'socialist who hates America' and accusing Biden of interfering in the 2022 election to elect him. He also urged the U.S. to exploit Brazil's rare earths to reduce dependence on China, referenced his father Jair Bolsonaro's imprisonment as a political sacrifice, and criticized Lula's opposition to Trump's policies in Venezuela, Iran, and Cuba.

item.title: Quem é Edilson Damião, novo governador de Roraima após renúncia de Denarium
item.description: Edilson Damião, a civil engineer and former vice-governor, assumed the governorship of Roraima on February 27, 2026, following the resignation of Antonio Denarium, who stepped down to run for Senate in the 2026 elections. Damião, who left the Republicans in March 2026 to join the União Brasil party and assumed its state presidency, stated he intends to run for governor in 2026 and is currently facing a cassation process at the TSE for alleged abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, alongside Denarium.

item.title: Nos EUA, Flávio Bolsonaro prega valores conservadores e promete lutar contra agenda woke
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, senator from PL-RJ, delivered one of his most conservative speeches since announcing his 2026 presidential bid, adopting an anti-system tone and promising to fight the 'woke' agenda. He positioned himself as a defender of traditional values and a critic of the political establishment.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro diz que país vive crise econômica e promete lutar contra agenda woke
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, a pre-candidate for president, declared Brazil is facing a devastating economic crisis and pledged to fight against the 'woke' agenda, radical environmentalism, and global elite interests. He positioned himself as 'Bolsonaro 2.0,' claiming both he and his father, Jair Bolsonaro, are victims of political persecution for defending conservative values, drawing parallels to the legal challenges faced by Donald Trump and Lula. The speech was delivered at CPAC in the U.S., amid efforts to soften his image to appeal to the Centrão and financial markets.

item.title: “Bolsonaro 2.0 será muito melhor”, diz Flávio sobre candidatura
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated that his 2026 presidential candidacy represents the continuation of his father Jair Bolsonaro’s government, claiming 'Bolsonaro 2.0 will be much better' due to gained experience. Speaking at CPAC in Texas, he criticized China, President Lula da Silva, the Brazilian judiciary, and accused the Biden administration of interfering in the 2022 elections to remove his father from power and install Lula. He also equated his father’s legal troubles with those of Donald Trump, alleging political persecution against conservative values.

item.title: Flávio pede monitoramento e pressão diplomática por ‘eleições justas’ no Brasil em discurso nos EUA
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) urged the United States to monitor freedom of speech in Brazil and apply diplomatic pressure to ensure 'free and fair' elections in 2026, speaking at CPAC in Texas. He accused the Biden administration of interfering in Brazil’s 2022 election to install Lula, claiming no proof exists, and compared his father’s legal struggles to Trump’s persecution in the U.S.

item.title: Governo Lula finaliza reforma na sala de jornalistas do Alvorada
item.description: The Lula government completed the renovation of the journalists' room at the Palácio da Alvorada after one year of work. The space is now more functional, featuring new outlets, benches, air conditioning, a water dispenser, and thermal coffee flasks, along with improved lighting and concealed wiring. Journalists who previously worked outdoors now have access to a covered, equipped space, although the bathrooms were not renovated.

item.title: Ex-presidente da Câmara defende que PT abandone postura defensiva e priorize engajamento nas redes
item.description: The former president of the Chamber of Deputies and PT pre-candidate for federal deputy, João Paulo Cunha, criticized the defensive stance of the Lula government and urged the party to take political leadership, especially on social media, to improve communication and voter engagement.

item.title: Moraes nega livre acesso de filhos a Jair Bolsonaro durante prisão domiciliar
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes denied the defense's request to expand visitation rules for Jair Bolsonaro during his temporary house arrest, maintaining fixed days and hours for non-resident children. The ruling emphasized that the measure is exceptional, temporary, and solely for health reasons, without altering the closed-regime sentence.

item.title: Com MDB na vice, Tarcísio praticamente enterra chance de partido apoiar Lula, dizem aliados
item.description: The affiliation of São Paulo's vice-governor, Felício Ramuth, with the MDB, orchestrated by Tarcísio de Freitas, strengthens the governor's base and diminishes the likelihood of the MDB supporting Lula nationally in 2026. The move also intensifies the conflict with Gilberto Kassab of the PSD, who sought the vice position to position himself as a state successor, but saw Ramuth choose to remain on the ticket, prompting the MDB to adopt a neutral stance.

item.title: Moraes nega pedido para filhos terem ‘livre acesso’ a residência onde Bolsonaro cumpre prisão domiciliar
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes denied the defense's request for Jair Bolsonaro's children to have 'free access' to the residence where he is serving house arrest. The decision maintains previous restrictions, limiting lawyer visits to weekdays with a maximum duration of 30 minutes and prior scheduling, and reaffirms that the benefit was granted solely for health reasons, without changing the sentence regime.

item.title: Zanin anula condenação de Garotinho por compra de votos no Rio de Janeiro
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin annulled Anthony Garotinho's conviction for vote-buying in the Chequinho operation, ruling that evidence obtained via USB drive without proper legal procedures was inadmissible. The decision, signed on March 27, 2026, followed a habeas corpus petition and relied on a 2022 precedent where the STF’s 2nd Chamber similarly annulled a conviction in the same operation due to irregular evidence collection. Zanin emphasized that the illegality of the evidence is not marginal but fundamental to the conviction’s validity.

item.title: Em crise, Lula desanda a falar bobagem e não se prepara para o resto de ano difícil que virá
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva was criticized for making superficial remarks about how technologies like cell phones, Pix, and credit cards facilitate spending, amid growing concerns over Brazil’s economic outlook. His comments were seen as unprepared for the difficult economic and social challenges ahead in late 2026, including inflation, unemployment, and fiscal pressures.

item.title: Moraes nega livre acesso de filhos a Bolsonaro em prisão domiciliar
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes denied the defense’s request for unrestricted access to Jair Bolsonaro by his non-resident children during his 90-day humanitarian house arrest, maintaining visitation restrictions. The house arrest was granted solely for health reasons (recovery from bronchopneumonia) and does not alter the closed-regime sentence. Moraes emphasized that Bolsonaro remains bound by closed-regime rules, and any violation will result in immediate revocation and return to prison or a prison hospital.

item.title: Moraes manda derrubar drones que sobrevoarem casa onde Bolsonaro está preso
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has banned drone flights within a 100-meter radius of Jair Bolsonaro's residence in Brasília, where the former president is under house arrest. The order authorizes police to seize or shoot down any drones that violate the judicial restriction.

item.title: PT define apoio a candidatura de João Campos na disputa pelo governo de Pernambuco
item.description: The PT in Pernambuco approved support for João Campos (PSB) in the gubernatorial race, with 59 votes in favor and 11 against. The alliance includes nominating Humberto Costa for the Senate and aims to strengthen a national bloc against the far-right, while increasing PT representation in the state legislature and federal Congress. Campos is expected to resign as mayor of Recife by Thursday to run for governor.

item.title: STF nega livre acesso a filhos de Bolsonaro durante prisão domiciliar humanitária
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes denied the defense’s request to expand visitation rights for Jair Bolsonaro’s non-resident children during his temporary humanitarian house arrest, maintaining fixed visiting hours on Wednesdays and Saturdays. The ruling emphasizes that the house arrest is exceptional, temporary, and solely for health reasons related to bronchopneumonia, without changing the closed-regime sentence. Violations may result in immediate revocation and return to prison.

item.title: Endividamento não é por pets, mas por corrupção, diz Caiado
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado (PSD), rebutted President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's (PT) claim that pet spending drives household indebtedness, asserting that corruption—not pets—is the real cause of rising living costs. Caiado accused Lula of governing with corruption for nearly 20 years and argued that dogs do not increase people's expenses.

item.title: Zanin suspende condenação de Anthony Garotinho e abre caminho para candidatura no Rio
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin annulled Anthony Garotinho's conviction for vote-buying in 2016, ruling that the evidence was illegally obtained. The decision clears the way for the former Rio governor to run in the 2026 elections by removing his ineligibility. The case will return to lower courts for a new trial without the illicit evidence.

item.title: MP libera até R$ 15 bi para exportadores afetados por conflitos
item.description: The Lula government has released R$15 billion in credit for exporters affected by international conflicts, including the Middle East war and U.S. tariffs, through BNDES and the Brazil Sovereign Plan. Funds come from the Export Guarantee Fund surplus and will support working capital, capital goods, and productive adaptation. As a provisional measure, it takes effect immediately but requires congressional approval within 120 days to remain valid.

item.title: Lei do Microcrédito sancionada por Lula abre nova fase para as microfinanças no Brasil
item.description: President Lula signed Law nº 15.364, which legally recognizes microfinance in Brazil, updating the PNMPO and distinguishing microcredit, productive oriented microcredit, and microfinance as separate concepts. The measure expands credit access for low-income entrepreneurs and includes purposes such as housing, health, vocational training, and accessibility, allowing authorized institutions and OSCIPs to operate within this expanded framework.

item.title: Zanin, do STF, suspende eleição indireta para governo do Rio
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Cristiano Zanin granted an injunction suspending the indirect election for Rio de Janeiro's interim governor, siding with the PSD's demand for a direct vote to choose who will govern the state until December 31, 2026. Zanin viewed Claudio Castro's resignation as an attempt to evade Electoral Justice and emphasized the need to uphold popular sovereignty and legal certainty, with final review pending before the full Court.

item.title: A ampla frente partidária formada pelo candidato de Bolsonaro ao governo do Rio Grande do Sul
item.description: Deputy Luciano Zucco (PL) succeeded in uniting the PL, PP, Republicans, Podemos, and Novo parties around his pre-candidacy for governor of Rio Grande do Sul, avoiding internal conflicts within the conservative field. The official launch is set for April 11 in Porto Alegre, with Flávio Bolsonaro in attendance, and the major ticket is taking shape, with state deputy Silvana Covatti (Progressistas) as the likely running mate.

item.title: QG de Flávio Bolsonaro quer empenho máximo de Nikolas Ferreira em sua campanha
item.description: Strategists of Flávio Bolsonaro’s pre-campaign urged the candidate to seek maximum effort from federal deputy Nikolas Ferreira as a key electoral operative, leveraging his strong positive image (46%) and massive social media reach (20 million followers) to attract younger voters and spread accessible messaging. Despite his past effectiveness — such as prompting the government to retreat on Pix taxation — Nikolas’ conservative stances on social issues, including his criticism of the bill criminalizing misogyny, create tension with Flávio’s attempt to appear more moderate than his extremist father. The strategy aims to balance Nikolas’ mobilization power against the risk of alienating centrist voters due to his polarizing positions.

item.title: Vice de Tarcísio, Felício Ramuth acerta filiação ao MDB após atritos com Kassab
item.description: São Paulo Vice Governor Felício Ramuth announced his affiliation with the MDB after leaving the PSD due to conflicts with Gilberto Kassab, national president of the party. The move comes amid a dispute over the vice-presidential spot on Governor Tarcísio de Freitas’ (Republicans) re-election ticket, which aims to retain the current composition. The shift strengthens the MDB’s role in the state ticket while weakening the PSD’s position.

item.title: Flávio nega conflito com Michelle e fala em “falsa narrativa”
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro denied any conflict with former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro, labeling speculation about internal family disagreements as a 'false narrative'. He stated that both are aligned on the same principle of avoiding what he considers a disaster for the country, though the quoted statement was cut off in the source text.

item.title: Fachin mantém decisão que suspendeu quebra de sigilo da empresa ligada a Toffoli
item.description: Supreme Court President Edson Fachin upheld the injunction suspending the breach of confidentiality of Maridt, a company linked to the family of Minister Dias Toffoli, stating that the presidency of the STF cannot individually review or hierarchically control monocratic decisions, except to preserve institutional coherence when a decision clearly deviates from the collegial orientation. Fachin also ordered Gilmar Mendes to justify his decision, which was issued in a habeas corpus related to the Pandemic CPI. The Senate's Organized Crime CPI criticized the move as limiting its constitutional investigative powers and announced it will appeal.

item.title: Efeitos da guerra no Brasil (por André Gustavo Stumpf)
item.description: The article discusses Ratinho Júnior's withdrawal from the presidential race due to pressure from his father, leading him to focus on family businesses. It highlights the PSD's internal choice between Ronaldo Caiado and Eduardo Leite as potential candidates, with Caiado tied to agribusiness and Leite seen as a moderate. It also links the current oil crisis, exacerbated by Donald Trump's policies, to Brazil's structural vulnerability, repeating historical patterns of external dependence and lack of planning to avoid economic impacts such as rising fuel and food prices.

item.title: CPMI do INSS termina sem relatório final
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Commission (CPMI) was closed without a final report after the rejection of deputy Alfredo Gaspar’s opinion by a 19-12 vote. Commission president Senator Carlos Viana declined to vote on the government’s alternative report but said investigations will continue and copies of the rejected report will be sent to the MPF and STF. The alternative report sought the indictment of 201 individuals, including former president Jair Bolsonaro.

item.title: O pavor de políticos que vão tentar golpe digital na eleição de outubro
item.description: The use of AI-generated deepfakes to create false videos of candidates such as Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro threatens to become a primary vector of disinformation in Brazil's October election. Experts warn that such manipulated content could be widely shared as truth, causing political panic and undermining the integrity of the electoral process.

item.title: Após romper com Kassab, vice de Tarcísio vai para o MDB
item.description: São Paulo's vice-governor, Felício Ramuth, announced his departure from the PSD and affiliation with the MDB following a break with party president Gilberto Kassab. Despite the split, Ramuth remains the favored choice of Tarcísio de Freitas to run as vice-governor in the 2026 elections. He thanked Kassab for his time in the PSD and stated the move aligns with Tarcísio's reelection project.

item.title: Geraldo Alckmin anuncia saída do Ministério do Desenvolvimento em 2 de abril
item.description: Vice President Geraldo Alckmin announced he will leave the Ministry of Development, Industry, Commerce and Services on April 2 to comply with electoral law requiring resignation from ministerial office to run for office. He will remain vice president but did not confirm whether he will stay on Lula's ticket or seek another position in the 2026 elections.

item.title: Vice de Tarcísio se pronuncia após entrar no MDB para disputar eleição
item.description: São Paulo Vice Governor Felício Ramuth announced his departure from the PSD and affiliation with the MDB to run for re-election alongside Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) in 2026. The move confirms the exclusion of Gilberto Kassab’s PSD from the gubernatorial ticket after months of growing tension between the governor and the party. Ramuth expressed gratitude for his time in the PSD and said the MDB offers a dialogue-oriented, solid environment for his campaign.

item.title: Sergio Moro escapou de armadilha pronta para tirá-lo das eleições
item.description: Sergio Moro avoided a political trap set by União Brasil and Progressistas, which aimed to leave him without a party label after the partisan window for Paraná races. After detecting the maneuver led by Ciro Nogueira, Antonio Rueda, and Ratinho Júnior, he joined Bolsonaro’s PL party to secure his candidacy. The move triggered backlash, including mayoral defections from the Bolsonaro camp, but Moro signaled openness to dialogue with Ratinho Júnior, amid underlying fears he might investigate state-owned companies if elected.

item.title: Vice-governador de SP, Felício Ramuth troca PSD pelo MDB e cita alinhamento com Tarcísio
item.description: São Paulo's Vice Governor Felício Ramuth announced his departure from the PSD and affiliation with the MDB, citing alignment with Governor Tarcísio de Freitas' political project. He thanked the PSD and highlighted MDB leadership — including Ricardo Nunes, Baleia Rossi, and Michel Temer — as an environment conducive to contributing to São Paulo and Brazil's development.

item.title: Vice de Tarcísio decide se filiar ao MDB após rompimento com Kassab
item.description: São Paulo's vice-governor, Felício Ramuth, has decided to join the MDB following a split with PSD national president Gilberto Kassab, who sought the vice-presidential spot on Tarcísio de Freitas' 2026 re-election ticket. Ramuth stated his decision aligns with the governor's project and came after a conversation with São Paulo mayor Ricardo Nunes (MDB), whose approval was required for the move. The vice vacancy is also being contested by Kassab and the PL, which had proposed André do Prado.

item.title: Zanin anula condenação de Garotinho na Operação Chequinho
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Cristiano Zanin overturned Anthony Garotinho's conviction in Operation Chequinho, ruling that the sentence relied on illegally obtained evidence without technical forensic analysis. The decision clears the former Rio de Janeiro governor of criminal liability for the alleged use of the Cheque Cidadão social program to buy votes in 2016.

item.title: STF cobra ação do governo Lula para aprovar projeto que limita atuação de militares na política
item.description: STF justices are urging the Lula government to act swiftly to get Congress to approve a bill limiting military involvement in politics, fearing a Bolsonarist return to power in the 2026 elections. They worry that if Flávio Bolsonaro defeats Lula, it could trigger a new wave of military infiltration into civilian institutions, which the Court views as a threat to democratic stability. The message was conveyed to PT leader Gleisi Hoffmann as part of efforts to build political support for the legislation.

item.title: De olho no 2º turno, Zema testa fôlego contra Flávio
item.description: Romeu Zema (Novo) has launched a national tour to consolidate his presidential candidacy, banking on his efficiency record and completed infrastructure projects in Minas Gerais. Despite right-wing fragmentation and strong competition from Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ), Zema holds 61% approval in Minas according to Paraná Pesquisas, while his vice governor Mateus Simões (PSD) assumes state leadership.
